matches.x: “[There is an] urgency of our coming to know God and His scheme of things, and of also developing within ourselves, our children, and our grandchildren a sense of history, including what God has done for Israel. Such reminders of the past-and millennia of memories from the scriptures-will help us amid present challenges. “For instance, one religious and political leader, Shez, had the difficult…

“[There is an] urgency of our coming to know God and His scheme of things, and of also developing within ourselves, our children, and our grandchildren a sense of history, including what God has done for Israel. Such reminders of the past-and millennia of memories from the scriptures-will help us amid present challenges. “For instance, one religious and political leader, Shez, had the difficult…
ידו בכול, “his hand will be against everyone;” a robber, a highwayman.
ידו בכול, “his hand will be against everyone;” a robber, a highwayman.

Kish In the Jaredite genealogy the son of Corom and Father of Lib (Ether 1:18-19). Kish inherited a peaceful kingdom from his father, reigned during a time of peace, and passed the kingdom on to his son (Ether 10:17-18).

Kish In the Jaredite genealogy the son of Corom and Father of Lib (Ether 1:18-19). Kish inherited a peaceful kingdom from his father, reigned during a time of peace, and passed the kingdom on to his son (Ether 10:17-18).

Hadad died, and Samla of Masreka reigned in his stead.
died, and ’Adath reigned in his stead, and the name of his wife was Maitabith, the daughter of 24 Matarat, the daughter of Métabédza’ab. These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom.

Where the Jaredites lived gives us another clue that more of them than Coriantumr alone must have interacted with the later people of Zarahemla or Nephites. It is commonplace for students of the geography of Book of Mormon events to suppose that the Jaredites dwelt only in the land northward. True, at one point in time centuries before their destruction, during a period of expansion, the Jaredite…

And Jared lived after he had begotten Hanok eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
And Serug lived after he had begotten Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
And Peleg lived after he had begotten Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.

LONGEST. Nikhsof (longest) is an infinitive in the nifal. It is similar to nilchom (fight) in or did he ever fight (nilchom) against them (Jud. 11:25).
